codeReview.summary: The feature correctly targets npm git-install behavior by committing prebuilt dist artifacts and adding coverage for first-run CLI flow without a manual build. However, the prepare-script guard currently masks real build failures whenever TypeScript is present, which can silently ship stale/broken dist output.

issues.description: `prepare` is `test -x node_modules/.bin/tsc && npm run build || true`, which returns success even when `npm run build` fails (because `|| true` swallows non-zero exits). This suppresses legitimate compile failures and can leave stale dist artifacts while install/prepare appears successful.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker had to research npm CLI issue #8440 at implementation time to justify the git-install workaround, but this known install-channel caveat is not captured in shared library docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows WebSearch/FetchUrl for npm issue #8440; `.factory/library` grep only finds a generic GitHub install command in `user-testing.md` and no note about devDependencies/prepare behavior for git global installs.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking prepare-script regression risk: build failures are currently swallowed. Aside from that, the implementation and tests align with the feature goal of GitHub shortcut install viability without global TypeScript/manual build.
